text: {"title":"Personal Baseline Deviation","group_name":"external_personal_baseline_deviation","family":"external_baseline_anomaly","summary":"Represent each row by how unusual its current lifestyle and physiology profile would look relative to individual day-to-day baselines learned from repeated auxiliary student observations, capturing acute departure from routine rather than only population-level healthiness.","depends_on":[],"strategy":"Use the auxiliary file only as an unlabeled repeated-measures source: drop health_condition, keep shared feature columns, group by student_id, and for each auxiliary student compute per-numeric medians and IQRs plus per-categorical modal value and modal frequency; clip each personal IQR to at least a small global-IQR floor to avoid division blowups. For every train or test row, compare its observed numeric values to each auxiliary student's baseline with mean absolute robust z distance abs(x - personal_median) / personal_iqr, and compare observed categoricals with off-mode penalties of 0 for modal match, 1 - modal_frequency for non-modal values seen for that student, and 1 for values never seen; ignore missing row fields in the distance denominator and emit an observed-field coverage feature. Select a small fixed nearest set of auxiliary personal baselines by this mixed distance and create distance-weighted summaries: nearest distance, mean weighted distance, numeric outside-personal-IQR rate, high-side outside rate, low-side outside rate, categorical off-mode rate, and coverage-adjusted anomaly score. If a row has too few observed fields, return neutral anomaly values plus the low coverage flag; never use auxiliary labels or project targets in these statistics.","expected_signal":"Students with similar absolute values can differ in risk if the profile looks routine versus a sharp within-person departure; these anomaly-style features may help distinguish broad at-risk middle cases from fit-like stable routines and unhealthy-like disrupted routines, improving class balance without target-derived encodings.","risk":"The auxiliary repeated-observation baselines may not transfer to the competition distribution, nearest-baseline summaries add computation, and the signal can be partly redundant with existing external support or temporal stability features if those already capture source-domain routine structure."}
